title: A bounded diagonal two-dimensional operator has an infinity-norm bound

source.notes: Generated from checked Lean ground truth; source repository withheld during public release.

informal.statement: If both diagonal coefficients are bounded in absolute value by rho, then the diagonal two-dimensional operator is bounded by rho in the two-entry infinity norm.
assumptions: the norm is max of the two absolute values
informal.expected_conclusion: The diagonal operator output norm is at most rho times the input norm.

declarations.name: vec2InfNorm
declarations.statement: def vec2InfNorm (x y : Real) : Real := max |x| |y|

declarations.name: diag2First
declarations.statement: def diag2First (a x : Real) : Real := a * x

declarations.name: diag2Second
declarations.statement: def diag2Second (b y : Real) : Real := b * y

declarations.name: diag2_infNorm_le
declarations.statement: theorem diag2_infNorm_le (a b rho x y : Real) (ha : |a| <= rho) (hb : |b| <= rho) (hrho : 0 <= rho) : vec2InfNorm (diag2First a x) (diag2Second b y) <= rho * vec2InfNorm x y

tasks.input: Translate the infinity-norm bound for a bounded diagonal two-dimensional operator into Lean 4.
tasks.target: theorem diag2_infNorm_le (a b rho x y : Real) (ha : |a| <= rho) (hb : |b| <= rho) (hrho : 0 <= rho) : vec2InfNorm (diag2First a x) (diag2Second b y) <= rho * vec2InfNorm x y

tasks.input: Complete the Lean proof using abs_mul, max_le, and coordinate bounds by the input infinity norm.
tasks.target: Unfold definitions, apply max_le, then use mul_le_mul with the coefficient and coordinate bounds.

tasks.input: Find the LeanNumerics coordinate-bound lemmas that motivate the diagonal infinity-norm proof.
